search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae conjunctivitis foreign body sensation red flags PDF
site:college-optometrists.org corneal abrasion foreign body sensation
site:college-optometrists.org eye referral chemical penetrating foreign body
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Irrigation and removal remain clinician-performed documentation only.
Medicine selection and doses were outside scope.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for foreign-body sensation in red-eye assessment, corneal abrasion, contact-lens context, and listed traumatic red flags; not exact for every retained ocular surface condition.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth red-eye history and first-contact eye assessment; procedures, diagnosis, medicine, and referral urgency remain clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: DHA red-eye guidance is direct for Dubai telehealth; College of Optometrists trauma and referral content requires UAE pathway review.
recency_verification: Current DHA Issue 2 guidance, College corneal-abrasion version 15, and the current official referral annex were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer DHA conjunctivitis issue, College corneal-abrasion version, or replacement referral annex was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports foreign-body sensation, grittiness, red eye, watering, and mechanical or chemical context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports visual loss, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, and post-traumatic warning prompts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports visual review, trauma, contact-lens, foreign-body-sensation, and assessed red-eye fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports inability-to-open-eye, worsening, and clinician-arranged follow-up documentation without automatic urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports foreign-body or contact-lens context plus pain, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports minor-injury, foreign-body and contact-lens context plus pain, blepharospasm, blurred vision, photophobia, watering, and redness.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports fluorescein and assessed corneal or foreign-body fields without a removal procedure.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of the involved area, fluorescein documentation, and referral when injury is deeper, contaminated, or possibly infected; medicine details are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports chemical, penetrating, embedded-foreign-body, laceration, and severe-abrasion warning prompts.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports severe eye pain and visual-loss warning context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

unresolved_source_gaps: Laterality, eyelid eversion, irrigation, foreign-body removal, broad supportive care, fixed same-day or 24-hour follow-up, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer a retained foreign body, abrasion, keratitis, chemical injury, or penetrating injury or generate drops, antibiotics, irrigation, removal, or referral advice.
Qualified UAE clinician review has not been completed.
